question: Describe the specimen testing procedures for the materials surveillance program.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, providing a critical barrier to fission product release. The RPV and its components are constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant’s design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the requirements and procedures for inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fasteners for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies the use of nondestructive examinations, controlled shipping environments, and quality assurance measures to ensure the integrity and safety of the reactor vessel. Key standards cited include the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code and ASME NQA-1 for quality assurance.

edges.description: Section A provides a general description of the reactor vessel and its role in the reactor system, while Section B references specific inspection and handling requirements for the reactor vessel, connecting the general component description to the detailed procedural requirements relevant to the materials surveillance program.
